name: brasil-futebol
target: iDotMatrix HXS-002 32x32 RGB panels over BLE

animations.flag.gif: Brazil bandeira with twinkling stars — complete 32x32 (use this in the carousel)
animations.flag_L.gif: left half of a 64-wide bandeira (pair with flag_R for one flag spanning BOTH panels)
animations.ball.gif: bouncing soccer ball on the green pitch

commands.carousel_both_panels: for MAC in 6F:5D:FE:85:89:31 1F:D6:5C:D2:8F:7F; do IDM_ADDR=$MAC python3 idm_push.py flag.gif:10 ball.gif:8 brasil.gif:8; done
commands.split_flag_across_panels: IDM_ADDR=6F:5D:FE:85:89:31 python3 idm_push.py flag_L.gif:300 ; IDM_ADDR=1F:D6:5C:D2:8F:7F python3 idm_push.py flag_R.gif:300
commands.gol_flash: for MAC in 6F:5D:FE:85:89:31 1F:D6:5C:D2:8F:7F; do IDM_ADDR=$MAC python3 idm_push.py gol.gif:8; done

notes: Pusher wipes all 12 slots, loads slots 0..N-1, then starts the on-device cycle. Up to 12 items.
One BLE central per panel at a time (single central).
On Pi/Linux (BlueZ) address by MAC (above). On macOS use the per-host CoreBluetooth UUID.
Never enable the device password (it locks the panel). Keep the vendor app off these panels.
